comment.body:
Knew NVDA was looking bullish… but yesterday’s commentary was concerning. Now we are back to a bounce into OPEX. I feel whipsawed.

Wednesday night was : “They need liquidity first. How? Bounce. Mean reversion, bringing vol down”.

Yesterday morning was “if market rips, that’s spot up/vol up = low liquidity”. And,

“Therefore, my current tactic is a short vol trade, as I written premarket, without any directional take.”

Friday morning now it’s “What did I say? I said, I’m leaning towards a bounce into OPEX…. I called a bottom at 5532”

You can say “don’t get caught up in bull/bear”, but a bounce into OPEX and a bottom at 5532 is bullish. If that is the case, then taking a directional long (bullish) trade at close yesterday would’ve been a profitable move. Or at least building a position in shares/calls for next week expiry.

Are you still holding on to calls expiring next Friday Alma? If so, that context would’ve been nice to know yesterday, but I took you at your word that you weren’t positioned in any direction. If you disclosed this, then your “why not disperse positions into both ways?” comment today would stand on a lot firmer ground.

This all rightfully leads to a lot of confusion. Great commentary and metrics as usual, but at the end of the day, we are all here to learn how to apply this information in a practical way to help make us more profitable.

While using this information to execute short term intraday trades has been exceptional via your signals, using this to execute longer trades has been challenging given the conflicting nature of your analysis at times like the last 3 days.

Hopefully this isn’t taken negatively, as I find Alma to be awesome… but this information is only useful if it can be applied successfully.
